old_claim.note: Industrial Capitalism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Consumerism is modeled as a market-oriented capitalist consumption order in which household spending, business sales, and production reinforce one another.

source_shape.source_locator: What Is Consumerism and Delving Deeper sections: Investopedia relates consumerism to market-driven consumption, consumer spending as an economic driver, people living in capitalist economies, business sales/revenue/profit, and the mid-20th-century positive framing of capitalism's standard-of-living benefits.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Investopedia frames consumerism around market-driven consumption, consumer spending, capitalist economies, business revenue, and mid-century capitalism claims.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports industrial capitalism as the market and business setting for consumerism while leaving room for other postwar institutions and policy drivers.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: consumerism remains enabled by capitalism_industrial, now with review-level source evidence.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to anti-consumer activism rather than a market-oriented consumption order.
A stronger source shows the scoped phenomenon is independent of capitalist market production and business sales.
A more precise postwar_consumer_capitalism node is added and replaces capitalism_industrial.
short_reason: Consumerism is modeled as a capitalist market-consumption order.
